meta.name: ECR-RS Attachment Scale
meta.fullName: Experiences in Close Relationships - Relationship Structures
meta.description: The ECR-RS is a 12-item self-report measure of adult attachment that assesses two fundamental dimensions: attachment avoidance and attachment anxiety in close relationships.
meta.author: Fraley RC, et al. (2011)
meta.reference: Fraley RC, Heffernan ME, Vicary AM, Brumbaugh CC. The Experiences in Close Relationships-Relationship Structures questionnaire. Psychological Assessment. 2011;23(3):615-625.
meta.instruction: Please read each statement and indicate how much you agree or disagree with it as it applies to your close relationships.

questions.text: It helps to turn to this person in times of need.
I usually discuss my problems and concerns with this person.
I talk things over with this person.
I find it easy to depend on this person.
I don't feel comfortable opening up to this person.
I prefer not to show this person how I feel deep down.
I often worry that this person doesn't really care for me.
I'm afraid that this person may abandon me.
I worry that this person won't care about me as much as I care about them.
I don't fully trust this person.
I worry a fair amount about losing this person.
It makes me mad that I don't get the affection and support I need from this person.

ranges.label: Secure Attachment
ranges.description: Your scores suggest a relatively secure attachment style with low avoidance and low anxiety. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue nurturing your close relationships
Your relational patterns appear healthy and adaptive
Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities

ranges.label: Moderate Attachment Concerns
ranges.description: Your scores suggest some attachment insecurity. Review subscale scores to understand whether avoidance or anxiety is more prominent. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Reflect on which relationships feel most secure and why
Practice open communication with trusted individuals
Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques
Stay in communication with people you trust

ranges.label: Significant Attachment Insecurity
ranges.description: Your scores suggest notable attachment insecurity that may affect relationship functioning. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Consider speaking with a therapist about relationship patterns
Explore attachment-focused therapy approaches
Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
Make sure you have trusted people around you for support
Follow the treatment plan recommended by your professional

avoidance.label: Low Avoidance
avoidance.description: You are comfortable with closeness and depending on others. This indicates that you are not currently showing significant post-traumatic stress reactions, and your psychological recovery is progressing well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your good psychological health
Stay emotionally connected with friends and family
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Remember to seek support if you face difficult events in the future

avoidance.label: Moderate Avoidance
avoidance.description: You sometimes find it difficult to open up or depend on others. Mild trauma-related reactions are normal after experiencing stressful events and typically diminish over time.
suggestions: Allow yourself time and space to process your emotions
Share your feelings with someone you trust
Maintain routines and structure in your daily life
Practice grounding techniques (such as focusing on your five senses) to stay present
Avoid repeated exposure to trauma-related information or content

avoidance.label: High Avoidance
avoidance.description: You tend to avoid closeness, self-disclosure, and depending on others. Your trauma-related reactions are quite pronounced and may be significantly impacting your functioning. Professional trauma-focused support is recommended.
suggestions: Contact a trauma therapy specialist as soon as possible
Process traumatic memories in a safe environment
Develop a safety plan and crisis coping strategies
Ensure you have a supportive social network
Follow professional guidance for a gradual recovery process

anxiety.label: Low Anxiety
anxiety.description: You feel relatively secure and do not worry excessively about relationships. This indicates that your anxiety levels are within the normal range, and your psychological state is relatively calm.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your healthy lifestyle and coping strategies
Practice daily relaxation activities such as deep breathing or stretching
Keep up a regular exercise routine
Cultivate mindful awareness habits
Maintain strong social connections

anxiety.label: Moderate Anxiety
anxiety.description: You sometimes worry about being abandoned or unloved. Mild tension or worry is common in everyday life. Simple self-regulation techniques can often help manage these feelings.
suggestions: Learn and practice progressive muscle relaxation
Try 5-10 minutes of mindfulness meditation daily
Reduce caffeine and stimulant intake
Record situations that trigger anxiety and try to analyze them rationally
Exercise regularly, at least three times a week

anxiety.label: High Anxiety
anxiety.description: You frequently worry about rejection, abandonment, and whether others truly care. Your anxiety level is elevated and may be accompanied by physical discomfort and persistent tension. Speaking with a professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a professional assessment as soon as possible
A combination of medication and psychotherapy may be beneficial
Learn to recognize and manage panic attacks
Avoid avoidance behavior; gradually face fears under professional guidance
Ensure you have a reliable support system
